debug_points.description: All 9 images show what appears to be a body of water (murky grey/brown water surface) with concrete or asphalt edges visible at the periphery. The camera appears to be positioned over water, possibly a canal, dock area, or flooded zone near Panama City given the coordinates. No road surface is visible in the main viewing areas to assess for potholes.

The images show a flooded street area in Panama City near coordinates 8.98, -79.52. The camera appears to be positioned in or near standing water/floodwater. Images 2 and 3 show cobblestone pavement along a curb edge with murky floodwater covering most of the roadway. No actual potholes with visible holes, missing asphalt, or exposed base material can be confirmed.

These images appear to be taken from an elevated or unusual angle looking down at a road surface. The visible pavement shows brick/paver surface in some views (NE, E) and asphalt in others. A manhole cover is visible in the south-facing view. No potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present in any of the 9 images.

The images show a large body of murky water (appears to be a flooded area or waterfront) adjacent to a paved road/sidewalk. The visible road surface shows cobblestone/brick paving and concrete curbing in good condition. No potholes are visible - the gray area dominating most frames is standing water, not road surface.

These images appear to be taken from an extremely low angle or malfunctioning camera, showing mostly dark/grey surfaces that appear to be close-up views of asphalt pavement. Images 1-3 show partial views of parked vehicles (silver/grey car visible). No actual potholes or road damage can be identified - the images show smooth asphalt surface without any visible holes, missing material, or exposed base layers.

These images appear to be taken from a very low angle or possibly from a camera mounted low on a vehicle, showing mostly the undercarriage view of parked vehicles and paved ground. The road surface visible is asphalt/concrete that appears wet or has some moisture but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible.

The images show a paved road surface with wet conditions. There is standing water visible across multiple angles, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present. The road surface appears intact though wet.

The Street View imagery appears to be captured from a very low camera position, possibly from a vehicle-mounted camera that was tilted downward or malfunctioning. Most images show only close-up views of parked vehicles (white and grey cars) and grey pavement surface. The road surface visible is smooth grey asphalt with no visible potholes, holes, or missing material.

These images show a flooded street corner with extensive standing water covering most of the road surface. The water is opaque and muddy, completely obscuring any view of the actual pavement beneath. While there is a curb, drainage grate, and sidewalk visible in image 5, no actual potholes with visible depth, broken edges, or exposed base material can be confirmed.

These images appear to be taken from an extremely low angle or possibly from a camera malfunction, showing mostly grey/gradient surfaces that appear to be either very close-up views of pavement or camera obstruction. The visible portions show asphalt surfaces, parked vehicles (red car visible in images 2, 3, 5; white car in images 5, 6, 7), and an orange traffic cone. No actual potholes or road damage with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth can be identified.

The images show a paved road surface with some moisture/wetness and scattered fallen leaves. There are dark wet patches and what appears to be oil staining on the asphalt, but no actual holes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible. The surface appears intact despite discoloration.

The images show a parking area with several parked vehicles. The road surface appears to be dark asphalt in generally fair condition. No potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are present in any of the 9 images.

These images appear to be taken from a position over water, likely a pier, dock, or waterfront area. The dark grey area dominating most frames is water, not road surface. The visible asphalt edges in some frames (images 2, 5, 6) show a concrete or paved surface at the water's edge in fair condition with some staining but no potholes.

These images appear to be taken from an extremely close/downward angle showing primarily asphalt road surface with some parked vehicles visible in the upper portions. The road surface shows normal wear patterns and minor surface texture variations but no actual holes or depressions with missing asphalt are visible.

The images show a paved road surface with what appears to be a large area of standing water or wet pavement obscuring most views. The visible asphalt in images 5 and 6 shows normal wear but no visible holes, depressions, or missing asphalt. A white/silver vehicle with yellow markings is visible parked nearby.

All images show a paved surface (appears to be a parking lot or similar area) with predominantly gray asphalt. The images are heavily obscured by what appears to be a gray overlay or rendering issue affecting the lower portions of most frames. No potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth are identifiable in any of the 9 views.

The images show a street view from what appears to be Panama City (based on coordinates). The road surface is wet with standing water pooling across the pavement, but there is no visible pothole - no hole with missing asphalt, no exposed base material, no broken edges or depth visible. The yellow taxi visible in images 5-7 shows typical urban street with wet conditions.

The images show a road surface with significant water pooling or wet conditions across most views. While there is standing water visible in multiple angles, no actual pothole with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or visible depth/broken edges can be confirmed. The water obscures the road surface but does not reveal any hole underneath.

The images show a paved road surface with white lane markings visible in several frames. There is significant water pooling or flooding covering most of the road surface in all directions, but no actual potholes with missing asphalt, exposed base material, or broken edges are visible. The water obscures the road but does not reveal any holes beneath it.
